mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because expanding employment raises aggregate wage income and consumption forecasts, directly lifting expected corporate revenues.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following month-over-month payroll increases are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A measured unemployment rate above 6% signals sufficient labor market slack to justify prolonged accommodative monetary policy, which compresses the equity risk premium and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and industrial production reflect a coordinated demand-supply expansion that reduces recession probability, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of the interaction term between RSAFS and INDPRO remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns fail to materialize in more than 50% of joint-increase months.

mechanism_narrative: During early-cycle recovery, month-over-month increases in industrial production signal reviving manufacturing activity and inventory restocking that boosts earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In early-cycle recovery periods defined by declining unemployment, month-over-month INDPRO increases are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% reflects a zero lower bound regime where central bank liquidity provision suppresses short-term discount rates and supports equity multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the consumer price index signal reflationary demand recovery that lifts nominal corporate revenues and reduces real debt burdens, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in housing starts and real GDP reveal a durable goods and construction-led economic expansion that raises cyclical earnings forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of the HOUST and GDPC1 joint-increase indicator remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month increases in the federal funds rate raise the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows and trigger capital reallocation into fixed income, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods of monetary tightening, month-over-month FEDFUNDS increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over any 5-year rolling window.
